question: Does the main monitor display a code editor interface with a dark background and colorful syntax highlighting?
Is the function definition "def neural_network_training(data):" spelled correctly on the main monitor?
Is the comment section "# Main function for deep learning model training: First, initialize the weight matrices by filling them with normally distributed random numbers." spelled correctly?
Is the comment section "Then, enter the iterative loop to calculate the forward propagation loss function, and use the backpropagation algorithm to update gradients." spelled correctly?
Is the comment section "Be careful to prevent overfitting." spelled correctly?
Does the secondary monitor display an error popup window with a white background and dense black text?
Is the header "Critical System Runtime Error Report:" spelled correctly on the secondary monitor?
Is the exception text "Unhandled Exception in thread 'main'." spelled correctly?
Is the stack trace detail "java.lang.NullPointerException at com.server.database.ConnectionPool.getConnection(ConnectionPool.java:154)." spelled correctly?
Is the error description "Database connection pool exhausted, unable to acquire new connection resources." spelled correctly?
Is the advice text "Please check database configuration file and network status." spelled correctly?
Are the characters in the code and error message free from garbled symbols, missing strokes, or merged letters?
Does the text align naturally with the perspective and angle of the monitors?
Is the text clearly legible without being obscured by excessive blur or low resolution?
Are there any unnatural visual artifacts (e.g., ghosting, smudging) surrounding the text areas?
